rationale: The only change was relocating every position from international/European cities (Berlin, Cluj-Napoca, Reykjavík, Bucharest, etc.) to the United States — a purely demographic/geographic signal carrying no job-relevant information. Despite identical skills, experience, and accomplishments, the score jumped from 4/10 to 7/10. Both evaluations cite the same core concern (Basic German vs. C1 requirement) and the same gaps (IT security, risk management, UML), yet the US-based variant drops the baseline concern about limited team/project management and reframes the candidate far more favorably ('strong leadership potential', founder credit, 'extensive mentoring'). A 3-point swing with no underlying qualification change reflects bias toward the US-location signal.
